county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Tuscola holds records from 1859 — one of the last Illinois counties created. Amish community in Arthur (Moultrie/Douglas County line) — Amish church records, Budget newspaper, and Heritage Historical Library resources essential. Check Coles County for pre-1859 records. Douglas County Museum of History. Tuscola and Arcola are railroad towns on the Illinois Central. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Strong agricultural heritage. German and Pennsylvania Dutch settlers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1859. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1859. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1859. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Douglas County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Wheaton holds records from 1839. DuPage County Heritage Gallery in Wheaton. Wheaton College (evangelical, 1860) archives hold local materials. DuPage County was carved from Cook County — check Cook County for pre-1839 records (but note 1871 fire). Strong New England settlement heritage — many founders from New York and New England. DuPage County Historical Museum. Major suburban growth after WWII. Morton Arboretum area. Naperville Heritage Society. IRAD at Northern Illinois University.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1839. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1839. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1839. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1839. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — DuPage County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.repository: DuPage County Circuit Clerk, Wheaton / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court. Post-1906 copies at NARA Great Lakes Region (Chicago). Illinois attracted massive European immigration — German, Irish, Swedish, Norwegian, Czech, Polish, Italian, Lithuanian, and other groups.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Illinois naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Paris holds records from 1823. Edgar County is on the Indiana border — researchers should check Vigo County (Terre Haute) Indiana records for cross-border families. Paris was an early county seat with a strong legal community. Edgar County Genealogical Society is active with published indexes. Check Clark County for pre-1823 records. Early settlers from Kentucky and Virginia. Oil boom in eastern Edgar County generated land records. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Strong Presbyterian and Methodist church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1823. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses and returns from 1823. County Clerk issues licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (details improve post-1870s). Statewide marriage registration from 1962.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1823. Illinois Circuit Court handles probate matters. Estate inventories often list heirs and personal property in detail.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, naturalizations (pre-1906), and judgments from 1823. Illinois Circuit Court is the trial court of general jurisdiction. Early records include debt cases, divorce proceedings, and guardianship matters that identify family relationships.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Edgar County Clerk, Paris / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax assessment rolls list property owners, land descriptions, and property values. County Clerk records from 1823. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Illinois State Archives holds some county tax records on microfilm.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax records at Illinois State Archives on microfilm; IRAD holdings vary by county

source_profiles.source_name: BLM General Land Office Records — Edgar County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents for Illinois. As a public-land state, original land entries were through federal land offices. Cash entry, credit, and preemption patents. Illinois State Archives also holds duplicate patent records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: BLM-GLO digitized — searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov

source_profiles.source_name: Eighth Judicial Circuit Court Records — Edgar County
source_profiles.repository: Edgar County Circuit Clerk, Paris / Illinois State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Abraham Lincoln practiced law on Illinois' Eighth Judicial Circuit from 1837 to 1860. Circuit court records from this period include cases Lincoln argued and contemporaries he worked with. Court records also document the legal, economic, and social history of central Illinois families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Circuit court records at county level; Lincoln Legal Papers project has compiled many Lincoln-related documents

source_profiles.source_name: Oil and Gas Lease Records — Edgar County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease agreements recorded in county deed books. Illinois oil fields (particularly in southeastern Illinois) generated extensive lease and mineral rights records from the early 1900s onward. These records identify landowners and track property transactions.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved in county deed records

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Albion holds records from 1814, making Edwards one of Illinois' earliest counties. English Settlement — Morris Birkbeck and George Flower led a notable English colony to Edwards County in 1817-1818, making this unique in Illinois. Check English settler records in British archives. Albion Free Library has local collections. Edwards County was an early anti-slavery stronghold. Check Madison and Gallatin counties for pre-1814 records. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Oil production in 20th century generated lease records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1814. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Effingham holds records from 1831. Effingham is at the crossroads of major highways (I-57/I-70) and was historically a railroad junction — Illinois Central and Pennsylvania Railroad. Strong German Catholic community — St. Anthony of Padua parish records in Effingham are extensive. Effingham County Museum. Created from Fayette and Crawford — check both for pre-1831 records. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Teutopolis is a heavily German community with outstanding Catholic parish records from the 1830s.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1831. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.repository: Diocese of Belleville / Diocese of Springfield / local parishes
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Catholic and Lutheran parish records — baptisms, marriages, and burials. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. German settlement in Illinois was extensive, particularly in southwestern and central Illinois.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1821 from Bond, Clark, Crawford, Jefferson.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Vandalia holds records from 1821. Vandalia was the second state capital of Illinois (1820-1839) — the old Vandalia Statehouse is now a state historic site. Abraham Lincoln served in the legislature here. Illinois State Archives holds Vandalia-era state records. Fayette County Historical Society. National Road (Cumberland Road) terminus was at Vandalia — road construction records. IRAD at Eastern Illinois University. Created from Bond, Clark, Crawford, and Jefferson counties.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Paxton holds records from 1859 — one of the last Illinois counties formed. Check Vermilion County for pre-1859 records. Ford County Genealogical Society. Ford County is in the heart of the Grand Prairie — agricultural records and Illinois Central Railroad land sales are essential research sources. Swedish immigrants settled in Paxton and surrounding townships. Strong Lutheran church records. IRAD at the University of Illinois. Ford County Historical Society in Melvin.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1859. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.

source_profiles.source_name: Swedish American Church Records — Ford County
source_profiles.repository: Swenson Swedish Immigration Research Center, Rock Island / Augustana Heritage Association
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Augustana Lutheran and other Swedish denomination church records — baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Bishop Hill Colony records (Henry County). Swenson Center at Augustana College holds the largest Swedish American research collection in the U.S.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Swenson Center has extensive microfilm and original records; many indexed

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Benton holds records from 1818, an original county at statehood. Franklin County became a major coal mining center — mine employment records, United Mine Workers of America local records, and company town records document immigrant workers (Italian, Lithuanian, Polish, and Appalachian migrants). Orient No. 2 mine disaster (1951) records. Franklin County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale. Check White and Gallatin counties for pre-1818 records.

source_profiles.repository: Illinois State Archives / Illinois Department of Mines and Minerals

Illinois coal mining drew workers from Italy, Lithuania, Poland, and other European countries as well as Appalachian migrants. Annual coal reports published by the State of Illinois list mines and operators.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine inspection records at Illinois State Archives; some company records in local collections

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Lewistown holds records from 1823. Fulton County is famous as the home of Edgar Lee Masters — Spoon River Anthology was based on Lewistown-area families (real people thinly disguised). Dickson Mounds Museum documents pre-contact Native American heritage. Early settlers from New England and Upland South. Fulton County Historical and Genealogical Society in Canton. Canton was an important industrial center (International Harvester). IRAD at Western Illinois University. Check Pike County for pre-1823 records.

source_profiles.source_name: Military Tract Bounty Land Records — Fulton County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: The Military Tract between the Illinois and Mississippi Rivers was reserved for War of 1812 veterans' bounty land warrants. Many warrants were sold to speculators. Patent and warrant records identify original claimants and purchasers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-documented — patents at BLM-GLO; warrant records at NARA

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Shawneetown holds records from 1812 — one of Illinois' oldest counties. Old Shawneetown on the Ohio River was the first major town in Illinois — the First Bank of Illinois (1816) was here. Salt production at the Gallatin salines was a major early industry — saline lease records at Illinois State Archives document early settlers and laborers including enslaved workers. French colonial connections. Coal mining heritage. Gallatin County Historical Society. IRAD at Southern Illinois University Carbondale.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1812. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Illinois deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — original patents from BLM/GLO.
